extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each category: I excluded all “By our Company,” “By our Directors,” “By our Promoters,” “By our Subsidiaries,” “By our KMPs” and “By our SMPs” rows. Among the “Against” rows, the company has 1 criminal and 4 tax proceedings, with nil statutory/regulatory proceedings; directors, promoters, subsidiaries, KMPs and SMPs have nil against them, so regulatory is 0 and tax is 4 for the company and subsidiaries. The quantified aggregate amount against the company is ₹36.55 million, while the subsidiaries’ amount is nil; therefore the group litigation amount is ₹36.55 million, converted to ₹3.655 crore, and the contingent-liabilities figure is the stated total of ₹6.00 million as at March 31, 2025, converted to ₹0.60 crore. The customer concentration figures are company-wide and use revenue from operations for full Fiscal 2025: top five is the stated top-four 78.42% plus the fifth customer’s 2.85% = 81.27%, and top ten is the sum of the stated top-four total and the remaining six percentages = 89.55%. The source tables use ₹ million; conversion to crore is by dividing by 10 (₹10 million = ₹1 crore).
